findings.problem: The chapter introduces postpartum stitches only retrospectively ("stitched flesh") after eliding the tear assessment/repair sequence, so a major bodily consequence is summarized away at the point where the scene should stay fully embodied. This is sanitization drift (summary replacing scene) in a high-consequence passage.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand the immediate postpartum block around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:211, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:213, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:215, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:221 to render Keane's tear assessment and repair decision/action in-scene (or an explicit deferred-repair rationale). Then align snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:313 so later pain/clot management clearly follows from what was already shown.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised text in the 211-221 block includes at least one explicit repair-related action and one explicit physical sensation tied to that action/decision, and line 313 no longer introduces stitches as new information. FAIL if repair remains implied only by retrospective mention.

findings.problem: Noreen's reassurance line ("You've done hard things before. This is one more and we do it together.") reads as generic endurance coaching and blunts her voice profile, which explicitly forbids saintly/generic support slogans.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the encouragement beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:99 (and, if needed, nearby setup at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:73) using Noreen's idiolect: colloquial contraction, concrete room-level sensory anchor, and her clipped under-pressure cadence instead of abstract motivational phrasing.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Noreen's revised support line in the 99-area includes a specific sensory/situational anchor from the room and avoids abstract pep-talk language (for example, "hard things"/"we can do this" formulas). FAIL if the line remains interchangeable with generic encouragement dialogue.

findings.problem: Across urgent exchanges, multiple spoken lines drift into full-form auxiliaries/negatives (e.g., "You should not... I will...", "He is not...", "does not...", "I will witness"), creating pockets of stiff formality against the chapter's high-contraction spoken baseline.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework spoken lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:117, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:271 toward contractions unless deliberate legal/clinical emphasis is intended and legible in context.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least three of those four targeted full-form constructions are converted to contractions, and any remaining uncontracted form reads as intentional emphasis. FAIL if multiple unmotivated full-form clauses remain in pressure dialogue.

findings.problem: Assent beats overuse a repeated "single nod" construction (see also lines 141 and 299), which reads as a recurring prose formula rather than a fresh physical response.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least two assent beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:65, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:141, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:299 with differentiated actions tied to scene logistics (for example, tool handling, door positioning, bike preparation, or eye-line shifts).
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two nod-based assent beats remain across those three locations and each retained assent beat is paired with a distinct physical action. FAIL if all three locations still resolve primarily as nod variants.

summary: Chapter 17 lands core causality (flood labor, husband absence, women-led delivery, and witnessed guardian designation) and stays continuity-consistent, but it fails craft/dialogue/prose at review threshold due one high-severity sanitization gap in postpartum embodiment, plus unresolved dialogue-register drift and a recurring prose gesture tic.
